column17: Chart Designer: Dr. Thomas R. Nicely

column2: The 1983 Tampa Bay Buccaneers season began with high expectations,  Some predicted then to be Super Bowl contenders, but disappointment quickly set in as personnel changes and a rash of injuries contributed to a league-worst 2-14 record.  The team lost it's starting QB in a contract dispute and lost it's top running back to a season ending injury. The team's record was somewhat deceptive; they lost numerous close games, but failed to execute at key times. According to McKay, "I think the other teams looking at us respect us. I don't think anyone is volunteering to play us".The Buccaneers took their opponents into overtime several times during a season that included a record number of overtime games league-wide. Nine of their losses were by a touchdown or less, and six by a field goal or less. Eighteen different players spent time on the injured reserve list over the course of the season, and only three started all sixteen games.
